verse_text: and perseverance, character; and character, hope.

links.context: Rejoicing: See [[LUK.6:22]], [[LUK.6:23]]. Suffering is not necessarily a sign of God's displeasure, as thought by many Jews (see [[LUK.13:1]]-4; [[JOH.9:1]]-3). Rather, Christians accept persecution joyfully, knowing that the rewards of the Kingdom far outweigh any sufferings on earth. See also [[ROM.5:3]]-5.

No longer remembers does not imply the faithful are to forget the Passion and Cross of Christ, any more than a woman "forgets" labor. Rather, we now see these sufferings in light of the victory of the Resurrection, and this victory transfigures our perception of sufferings. Christ's victory allows us to rejoice in anguish because of the infinitely greater good that comes from it ([[ROM.5:3]]-5; [[PHP.3:10]]).
